source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: Closet Murdock Main
archetype.receipt: 53.9% over 135 games on Murdock — about 3.7 more wins per 100 games than the average Murdock player. The wide pool hides a weapon.

entries.receipt: 53.9% over 135 games on Murdock vs 51.7% overall

entries.change: Queue offlane instead of your average mix
entries.receipt: 53.0% there vs 51.7% overall, 133 games of evidence

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

plan: Queue offlane as primary. It is your best role at 53.0% over 133 games (your overall is 51.7%). Set support as secondary, not the other way around.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Murdock. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 3.7 more games per 100 on it.
Your champion pool is 12+ heroes wide and your top three are only 41% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
The math: you are at 889 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 855.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: Xeebs brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Xeebs is 54% over 235 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 1 pentakill in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 19 (plus 12 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Mr_Meat31 has receipts.

insights.title: Your lean, relative to the squad: objective damage
insights.finding: Mr_Meat31's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is objective damage at 0.8x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.
